title: Bad Apple ESP32 Code Maker
summary: Python utility that converts video into a C array for ESP32 composite output.
description: A quirky developer utility for retro‑style ESP32 demos: feed it a video and BadAppleESP32CodeMaker slices it into frames, encodes them as a C array and bundles everything you need to flash the animation onto an ESP32. Ideal for makers who want to display moving graphics without filesystem support, it combines a Python conversion script with a little ESP32 player to bring cult‑classic footage straight to your microcontroller.
